title: Crystal Nucleation and Growth Kinetics
content: Crystallisation proceeds via two stages: nucleation (formation of stable nuclei; rate J = A·exp(−ΔG*/kT) where ΔG* is the free-energy barrier) and crystal growth (rate proportional to supersaturation σ = (C−Cs)/Cs). Controlled cooling rates suppress spurious nucleation, producing fewer, larger crystals. The Tammann curve shows that maximum nucleation rate and maximum growth rate occur at different temperatures, enabling zone-controlled crystallisation profiles.
